# Research: main
<!-- Generated by /speckit.plan -->
<!-- Date: 2026-04-06 -->

## Resolved Clarifications

### CORS wildcard + credentials
The CORS fairing at `src/fairings/cors.rs` sets `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` with `Allow-Credentials: true`. Per the Fetch spec, browsers should reject this combination. However, since the frontend is served from the same origin (embedded in binary), CORS headers are irrelevant for same-origin requests. This only matters if the API is accessed from a different origin (e.g., a separate frontend). **Resolution:** Low priority — leave as-is for now; revisit if PostgreSQL migration involves separating frontend and backend.

### Debt amount type (i32)
Debt amounts use `i32` in the model (`src/model/debt.rs:11`), allowing a max of ~2.1 billion cents (~21M EUR). For a 2-user household app, this is more than sufficient. **Resolution:** No action needed.

### PostgreSQL branch in main.rs
Lines 35-48 in `src/main.rs` handle PostgreSQL by dropping and recreating the database on every startup. This is clearly development-only code that was never fixed for production use. The MySQL branch (lines 27-34) correctly uses `CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS`. **Resolution:** Fix PostgreSQL branch to match MySQL pattern before migration.

### SeaORM PostgreSQL support
`Cargo.toml` only includes `sqlx-sqlite` feature. Adding `sqlx-postgres` is required for PostgreSQL support. All migrations use SeaORM's database-agnostic API, so they should work on PostgreSQL without changes. The `migration/src/lib.rs` registers all migrations generically.

### Transaction timestamp handling
- Model: `src/model/transaction.rs:12` — `pub timestamp: i32`
- Service struct: `src/services/group.rs:37` — `pub timestamp: u32`
- Service creation: `src/services/group.rs:390-400` — casts timestamp via `SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_secs() as i32`
- Route request: `src/routes/group.rs:19` — `timestamp: Option<u32>` in `TransactionCreationRequest`
- Frontend type: `client/src/data/Types.tsx:26` — `timestamp: number` (JS number, no change needed)
- Migration column: `migration/src/m20220920_234028:23` — `.integer()` (need new migration to alter to `.big_integer()`)

### Transaction update design
Current `delete_transaction` in `src/services/group.rs:315-339` validates group membership and creditor ownership. An update function should follow the same pattern: verify group membership, verify creditor, then update transaction fields and recalculate debts (delete old debts, create new ones with the splitting algorithm).

### Group deletion design
The `is_owner` field in `group_member` (model at `src/model/group_member.rs:11`) already tracks ownership. Cascade deletes are defined in migrations: group → group_member (cascade), group → transaction (cascade), transaction → debt (cascade). So deleting the group row will clean up everything automatically.

### User profile editing design
The `User` service struct at `src/services/user.rs:11-15` has `id`, `username`, `nickname` (password stripped). The SeaORM model at `src/model/user.rs` has all fields. An update endpoint should allow changing `nickname` and optionally `password` (re-hash with bcrypt if provided).
